meta.title: HSE Marine & Offshore — Safety Case Argument Map
meta.subtitle: Platform Alpha, safety-case demonstration extract
meta.author: HSE Marine & Offshore
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This extract presents the safety-case argument for Platform Alpha as a goal-structured claim → argument → evidence map. The top claim is that major-accident hazards are controlled to ALARP. The argument is laid out as a hierarchical table plus the supporting evidence list.
Top claim: Major-accident hazards on Platform Alpha are controlled so that risks to personnel are reduced to As Low As Reasonably Practicable (ALARP).

rows: Show passive + active fire protection adequacy
Demonstrate TR endurance and EER provision
sections.caption: Goal-structured safety-case argument — claim / sub-claim / strategy / evidence / status

items: Bowtie analyses for all major-accident hazards (BT-01 to BT-06)
HAZOP and HAZID study reports with closed actions
Fire & explosion analysis FEA-03 with detector and deluge mapping
Temporary refuge endurance and escape-route survivability assessment
Structural integrity management plan and latest inspection report

rows.title: EER sub-claim not fully supported
rows.description: Evacuation, escape and rescue endurance carries an open action pending the updated EER assessment.

rows.title: Bowtie barriers verified
rows.description: Hydrocarbon-release prevention and mitigation barriers are evidenced and current.

rows.control: Fire & gas detection with automatic deluge

rows.action: Close the EER endurance open action with the updated assessment
Re-verify all bowtie barrier owners before the next safety-case revision

sections.text: The ALARP demonstration is not complete until the EER sub-claim open action is closed.
